reason:
The agent successfully initiated the GitHub username lookup workflow but encountered a 502 "Failed to fetch access token" error from the GitHub proxy and was unable to retrieve and return the GitHub username as requested.
  Bugs found:
    - GitHub proxy returned a 502 "Failed to fetch access token" error when the agent attempted to fetch the authenticated user information, preventing the retrieval of the GitHub username.
steps: Navigated to http://localhost:47891 — Page loaded successfully with the SuperAgent application and sidebar visible showing multiple agents.
Clicked on the "QA-20260502-174724-776q" agent in the sidebar — Agent page loaded successfully, showing the agent detail view.
Verified agent status "idle" in the status indicator — Agent status was confirmed as "idle" (step 3 requirement satisfied).

Clicked on the message input field and typed "Use the GitHub tool to check who I am. Tell me my GitHub username." — Message was successfully entered in the input field and the Send button became enabled.
Clicked the "Send message" button — Message was submitted successfully, agent status changed to "working", and a new session titled "GitHub Username Verification Request" was created.
Waited ~10 seconds and an "Account Access Request" card appeared asking to grant GitHub account access — A GitHub account was already selected in the card, showing it was previously connected.
Clicked the "Allow Access (1)" button to grant GitHub access — Permission was granted, card transitioned to show the connected account status, and agent status changed back to "working".
Waited ~15 seconds and an "API Request Review" card appeared asking to allow the GitHub API call "GET /user" — This was an additional permission check for the actual API request.
Clicked "Allow" button on the API Request Review card — Permission dialog appeared with options to "Allow Once", "Always allow user", "Always allow read:user", or "Always allow all github requests".
Clicked "Allow Once" button in the permission dialog — Dialog closed and agent resumed processing, status remained "working".
Waited ~15 seconds and another API Request Review card appeared for retrying the GitHub user fetch — Agent was retrying the API call after the first attempt.
Clicked "Allow" and then "Allow Once" again on the second retry permission dialog — Dialog closed and agent continued processing.

Waited ~15 seconds and additional Bash execution cards appeared showing "Check connected accounts env var" and "Retry GitHub user fetch after brief delay" — Agent was attempting multiple approaches to retrieve the username.
Clicked "Allow" and "Allow Once" on another API Request Review card for the retry — Permission was granted.

Waited ~10 more seconds for final response — Agent completed work after 1m 38s total and returned an error response stating: "The GitHub proxy is returning a 502 'Failed to fetch access token' error, so I can't retrieve your username right now. This usually means the OAuth token for the connected account couldn't be refreshed. Could you try reconnecting the GitHub account, or would you like me to retry in a moment?"

Reviewed final response — Response did NOT include a GitHub username (step 7 requirement failed). The agent explained it could not retrieve the username due to a 502 proxy error with token refresh, and offered options to reconnect or retry.
bugs: GitHub proxy returned a 502 "Failed to fetch access token" error when the agent attempted to fetch the authenticated user information, preventing the retrieval of the GitHub username.
